name: Tommy Ladnier
abstract: Thomas James Ladnier (May 28, 1900 – June 4, 1939) was an American jazz trumpeter. Hugues Panassié – an influential French critic, jazz historian, and renowned exponent of New Orleans jazz – rated Ladnier, sometime on or before 1956, second only to Louis Armstrong.

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: before, you mentioned one other trumpet player who you feel had an influence on you. Tommy Ladnier.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I heard regularly McKinney's Cotton Pickers Band every Monday night at the Greystone, and Fletcher Henderson's band came through with Coleman Hawkins and people like that in there -- and Tommy Ladnier, Kaiser Marshall.

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Dance
about_quotes.evidence: And then of course there had been jazz musicians like Sidney Bechet and Tommy Ladnier and Lord Hopkins. They’d been to Europe with different groups and shows and things like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Dance interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: And I used to hang out in a place, we used to call it, 'The House of Meditation'. With Bechet and Tommy Ladnier. They lived there. We used to all hang out at this spot

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: We all went down to the Chicago Armory. I can name every member of that band right now... a fellow named Tommy Ladnier playing third trumpet

about_quotes.speaker: Happy Caldwell
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, Leonard Hannon Davis, Jimmy Harris on trombone, Tommy Ladnier on trumpet... Benny Carter was on alto... Cy St. Clair was on bass
